NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO VACATE A VOID ORDER DATED JULY 18, 2018, CALENDAR OF NOV-01-2018 TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. ZENAIDA GRAVES' ("GRAVES") MOTION TO VACATE A VOID ORDER DATED JULY 18, 2018 IS DENIED. GRAVES HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE ORDER IS VOID DUE TO LACK OF NOTICE OR FRAUD. GRAVES APPEARED AT THE COURT HEARING ON JULY 2, 2018 AND HAD AN OPPORTUNITY TO OBJECT TO SURETEC'S REQUEST THAT THE COURT GRANT ITS PETITION. THE FACT THAT THE COURT ULTIMATELY USED AN ORDER WHICH HAD BEEN SUBMITTED AT THE PROBATE EXAMINER'S REQUEST IN JANUARY, 2018 - INSTEAD OF THE ORDER THAT SURETEC CIRCULATED TO THE PARTIES FOLLOWING THE JULY 2, 2018 HEARING - HAS NO BEARING ON THE VALIDITY OF THE ORDER. GRAVES HAS ALSO FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE FRAUD OR ANY OTHER GROUND UPON WHICH THE COURT COULD DETERMINE THAT THE JULY 18, 2018 ORDER IS VOID.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions In The Form Of Monetary Penalties Under Code Civ Proc 128.7 Against Harald Westendorf And For An Award Of Expenses And Attorneys Fees Against Alexis D . Padlan And In Favor Of The Blandina D. Padlan Trust
Off Calendar Jul-17-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Harald Westendorf, Esq. appearing by CourtCall for Alexis Padlan.
Joana Mia Perez-Arroyo, Esq. appearing by CourtCall (limited scope) for Zenaida Graves.

The motion is called, argument is held. The Court addressed the proof of service. Ms. Perez-Arroyo stated that she did not have the proof of service noting that she was only hired for today's motion.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling: Defendant and Trustee Zenaida Graves' Motion for Monetary Sanctions Under CCP 128.7 Against Attorney Harald Westendorf and Motion for Expenses and Fees Against Alexis Padlan and in Favor of the Blandina D. Padlan Trust is DENIED. "A motion for sanctions under [CCP 128.7] shall be made separately from other motions or requests. . . . Notice of motion shall be served as provided in [CCP] Section 1010, but shall not be filed with or presented to the court unless, within 21 days after service of the motion, or any other period as the court may prescribe, the challenged paper, claim, defense, contention, allegation, or denial is not withdrawn or appropriately corrected." (CCP, 128.7, subd. (c)(1).) The record contains no evidence that Moving Party served the notice of motion consistent with subdivision (c)(1)'s "safe harbor" provision. Further, "[t]he law is well settled that the [CCP 128.7] safe harbor provisions preclude a party from seeking monetary sanctions for an offending pleading following a dispositive ruling by the trial court on the pleading." (Malovec v. Hamrell (1999) 70 Cal. App. 4th 434, 441, citing Goodstone v. Southwest Airlines Co. (1998) 63 Cal.App.4th 406; Cromwell v. Cummings (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th Supp. 10; Barber v. Miller (9th Cir.1998) 146 F.3d 707; Ridder v. City of Springfield (1997) 109 F.3d 288.) Here, the Court has already made a final determination and ruling on the underlying petition. Additionally, to the extent the motion is based on discovery activities, it is denied as improper. (See CCP 128.7, subd. (g) ["This section shall not apply to disclosures and discovery requests, responses, objections, and motions."].) Independently, the motion is denied as without merit. Petitioner Alexis Padlan's request for fees and costs is DENIED. Considered under CCP 128.7, subdivision (c)(1), the request for "$2,000 for having to defend the June 5, 2019 motion to set aside the Court's orders and the present motion" is overbroad to the extent it includes costs and fees for the underlying motion and not merited for the 1.5 page "response" on this motion and, in any event, unsupported by evidence. Considered under CCP 128.7, subdivision (h), the request is denied as improper because such a request must be made in a properly served and filed "motion for sanctions." (See CCP 128.7, subd. (h).) Mr. Westendorf is to prepare a form of order.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR #13714, Tel. 415.601.7054, Email: apourtabib@gmail.com. Deputy: C. Kabasares.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order Re: Petition To Subject Trust To Court Supervision, Remove Trustee, Compel Accounting, And Attorneys Fees Entered 3-14-19
Off Calendar Jun-05-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mia Perez, Esq. (limited scope) for and with Zenaida Graves, Respondent/Moving Party.
Harald Westendorf appearing by CourtCall for Alex Padlan, Petitioner.

Matter called. Argument on Zenaida Graves' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order Re: Petition To Subject Trust To Court Supervision, Remove Trustee, Compel Accounting, And Attorney's Fees Entered 3-14-19 is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling: this Motion to Set Aside Order Re: Petition to Subject Trust to Court Supervision, Remove Trustee, Compel Accounting, and Attorney's Fees Entered March 14, 2019 refers to CCP 473(b), 663, and 657. Although the Motion is captioned only as a motion to set aside the March 14, 2019 Order, the Court considers this as a motion for reconsideration. The other cited statutes are inapplicable. Per CCP 1008, an application to reconsider and modify or vacate an order must be brought within 10 days of service of the order, and be based upon new or different facts, circumstances, or law. Respondent did not comply with any of the forgoing. The Motion is denied.

Mr. Westendorf stated that the motion is frivolous, requested additional orders by the Court as set forth on the record, and also requested sanctions in the amount of $1,000.00. The Court stated that it would not do anything sua sponte.

Mr. Westendorf is to prepare a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-20-2018 Continued To Nov-05-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Harald Westendorf appeared by telephone for Alexis Padlan.
James Curran for Suretec Insurance Company.
Trisha Friedeberg (Former Attorney for the Trustee).
Zenaida Graves in Propria Persona.

Mr. Westendorf stated that an accounting was filed, that the property was sold and that there is no concern about removing Ms. Graves at this point. Mr. Westendorf also stated that the remaining issue is surcharge or compensation for use of the property by Ms. Graves for over ten years, that his Client put out over $15,000.00 in Attorney's fees, that he is asking Ms. Graves to waive Trustee's fees and pay for Mr. Padlan's Attorney's fees, and suggested an evidentiary hearing. Ms. Graves stated that there was no self dealings, that the Beneficiaries agreed to her remaining in the house, that she paid the mortgage, that this petition should not have been filed and that the Beneficiary should have talked to her. Mr. Westendorf stated that he can provide something in writing and the Court can make a determination, that he does not have an expert on rental values, that they would be providing numbers based on opinions and that he can submit briefs on the costs issue they feel Ms. Graves should be charged for. The Petition for Entry of Judgment Pursuant to Terms of Stipulated Settlement agreement, etc., Petition to Subject Trust to Probate Court Supervision, etc., and First and Final Account, etc., are continued to November 5, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Westendorf is to file his additional briefs by September 19, 2018. Ms. Graves is to file her response by October 19, 2018.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition To 1) Subject Trust To Probate Court Supervision; 2) Remove Trustee, Appoint Successor Trustee; 3) Compel Accounting; 4) Authorize Payment Of
Off Calendar May-22-2017 Continued To Jul-24-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Harald Westendorf appeared by telephone for Alexis Padlan.
Charles Perry appeared by telephone for Trustee Gzenaida Padlan Grades who was present in Court.

Mr. Westendorf stated that the parties have been working together, that the house sold in January, that the accounting still is not forthcoming and that there are $645,000.00 in liquid assets. Mr. Westendorf also stated that there is not a great deal of trust between his Client and the current Trustee, spoke of the Trustee taking out a loan and advancing funds, suggested a bond or a blocked account for security and requested setting 60-days for compliance. Mr. Perry stated that he has no objection to reporting on the accounting, that they have some concerns about the CPA and are looking into others, and that Counsel has a draft of the accounting, questions, bank statements and the closing statement. Mr. Perry also stated that he prefers a bond rather than a blocked account, that the Trustee has not posed any risk to the Estate or the Trust, spoke of the Trustee maintaining the property and time spent, and asked if distribution can be made to reduce the amount of bond. The Court set bond in the amount of $650,000.00. Status on filing of bond is set for June 19, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition to Subject Trust to Court Supervision, etc. is continued to July 24, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the accounting.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Tim Kneis. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.
